q: Biology is broadly divided into five major themes: organization, information, energy and matter, interactions and evolution.
The hierarchy of life on earth is unfolded from atom to biosphere.
With each set up, new properties emerge (emergent properties) because of interactions among components at the lower levels.
A correlation of structure and function can be observed at each level of the biological hierarchy.
The hummingbird's anatomy allows the wings to rotate at the shoulder, which is a unique ability among birds to fly backward or hover in place.
The cell is the lowest level of organization that can perform all activities required for life.
Eukaryotic cells contain membrane-enclosed organelles, including DNA in nucleus.
Prokaryotic cells contain membrane-enclosed organelles.
The genetic information is encoded in the nucleotide sequences of DNA.
DNA transmits heritable information from parents to offspring.
DNA sequences (called genes) act as instructions to make a cell's protein production by being transcribed into mRNA and then translated into specific proteins.
Gene expression refers solely to the process of translating mRNA into proteins.
Gene expression can produce some RNAs that are not translated into proteins but serve other important functions.
All organisms require energy for their life.
Producers convert energy from sunlight to chemical energy and some of which is then passed to consumers.
Energy flows through the ecosystem, usually entering as light and exiting as heat.
Chemical cycle occurs between organisms and the environment.
In feedback regulation, a process is regulated by its output or product.
In negative feedback, accumulation of the product slows its production.
In positive feedback, a product speeds up its own production.
Feedback is a type of regulation common to life at all levels, from molecules to ecosystems.
Evolution is the process of change that has transformed life on Earth and accounts for the unity and diversity of life.
Domain Bacteria and Archaea consist of prokaryotes.
Domain Eukarya includes various groups of protists as well as fungi, plants, and animals.
Bacteria are the most diverse and widespread prokaryotes and are now classified into multiple kingdoms.
Protists are mostly unicellular eukaryotes.
Kingdom Plantae consists of multicellular eukaryotes that carry out photosynthesis, the conversion of light energy to the chemical energy in food.
Kingdom Fungi is defined in part by the nutritional mode of its members, which absorb nutrients from outside their bodies.
Kingdom Animalia consists of multicellular eukaryotes that ingest other organisms.
Darwin (1809-1882) proposed natural selection as the mechanism for evolutionary adaptation of populations to their environments.

Natural selection is defined as the evolutionary process that occurs when a population is exposed to environmental factors that consistently cause individuals with certain heritable traits to have greater reproductive success than do individuals with other heritable traits.
Biology helps us to understand the living world and the ways its many species (including humans) function, evolve, and interact.
Agriculture is defined to produce commodities which maintain life, including foods, fibers, forest products, agricultural crops, horticultural crops, and their related services.
Myanmar's agricultural exports include rice, maize, blackgram, greengram, pigeonpea, chickpea, sesame, onion, tamarind, raw rubber, vegetables, and fruits.
Rice ($\textit{Oryza\ sativa}$) production is based on its environment, resulting in rain fed lowland rice, winter rice, deep-water rice, upland rice and irrigated rice.
The monsoon season is the main rice production season in Myanmar as rice paddies rely on copious amounts of water.
The traditional method for cultivating rice is flooding the fields while, or after, setting the young seedlings.
Wheat ($\textit{Triticum\ aestivum}$) is a grass widely cultivated for its seeds, a cereal grain that is a worldwide staple food.
The zero-tillage technique is a new method of wheat cultivation that is used in the Rice-Wheat cropping system.
Maize ($\textit{Zea\ mays}$) is a tropical plant that prefers warm humid weather.
Maize is a short-day plant and it is very sensitive to excess or deficit soil moisture.
Among the ASEAN countries, Myanmar is the leading pulse and bean producer.
Myanmar is the third-largest producer of pulses after India and Canada.
Horticulture is a branch of agriculture defined as the applied science of cultivating and growing plants used for both consumption and aesthetic or ornamental purposes.
The word horticulture is derived from the Latin words hortus and cultura, which mean garden and cultivation.
Horticulture does not include the intensive crop farming and large-scale crop production or animal husbandry.
Olericulture is the vegetable growing, dealing with the culture of non-woody (herbaceous) plants for foods.
Pomology or Fruticulture is the production of fruits and nuts.
Viticulture is the production of grapes, largely intended for winemaking.
Floriculture is the growing and marketing of flowers and ornamental plants for floristry.
Turf Management is the production and upkeep of turf, artificial and live, for use in recreation.
Arboriculture is the cultivation and care of individual trees, shrubs, vines, and other perennial woody plants.
Landscape Horticulture is the selection, production and care of plants used in landscape architecture.
Postharvest Physiology is the management of harvested horticultural crops to determine the best storage and transportation conditions to optimize shelf life after harvest.
Hydroponics is a method of growing plants without soil.
Hydroponic growing allows for faster growth and higher yields than traditional soil-based systems.
Hydroponics uses more water than traditional soil-based systems.
Roots in hydroponic systems may be physically supported by an inert medium such as perlite, gravel, or other substrates.
Roots in inert media can cause changes of the rhizosphere pH and root exudates can affect rhizosphere biology and physiological balance of the nutrient solution.
$\textit{Arabidopsis}$ serves as a model organism in plant science and genetics.
Aquaculture, also known as aquafarming, is the farming of aquatic organisms such as fish, crustaceans, molluscs, algae and other aquatic plants.
Aquaculture involves cultivating freshwater, brackish water and saltwater populations under controlled or semi-natural conditions.
Commercial fishing is the harvesting of wild fish.
Fish farming or pisciculture involves commercial breeding of fish, usually for food, in fish tanks or artificial enclosures such as fish ponds.
Mariculture or marine farming is a specialized branch of aquaculture involving the cultivation of marine organisms for food and other animal products.
Offshore mariculture refers to cultivation in enclosed sections of the open ocean.
Inshore mariculture refers to fish farms built on littoral waters.
Onshore mariculture involves artificial tanks, ponds, or raceways which are filled with seawater.
Shrimp farming is an aquaculture business that exists in either a marine or freshwater environment.
Oyster farming is an aquaculture (or mariculture) practice in which oysters are bred and raised mainly for their pearls, shells and inner organ tissue.
Algaculture is the farming of algae species including microalgae and macroalgae.
Integrated fish farming is a system of producing fish in combination with other agricultural or livestock farming operations centered on the fish pond.
The term Livestock refers to any domesticated, land-living animal that is raised to provide resources like meat, milk, eggs, and feathers.
The most common livestock animals are cattle, sheep, goat, pig, horse, chicken, and duck.
Livestock produce a range of fibre or textiles, such as wool and mohair.
Manure from livestock can be spread on fields to increase crop yields.
Manure is also used to make plaster for walls and floors and can be used as a fuel for fires.
The blood and bone of animals are also used as fertilizer.
Animals such as horses, donkey, and yaks can be used for mechanical energy, still used in plowing fields and transporting goods.
The grazing of livestock is sometimes used as a way to control weeds and undergrowth.
Livestock contribute to the conservation of biodiversity and genetic resources for food and agriculture.
Plant only is able to manufacture food through the process of photosynthesis.
Food may be classified into groups like heat or energy producing, body-building, protective, and luxury food.
The bacterium $\textit{Lactobacillus}$ helps in the formation of curd from milk.
Yeast, one of the simplest eukaryotes, is used in the process of fermentation.
The microorganisms present in the soil act as a decomposing agent, which assist in the production of the compost from dead and decaying organic matter.
Pharmacognosy is a name derived from Greek words which mean drug and knowledge.
Pharmacognosy is defined as the study of the physical, chemical, biochemical and biological properties of drugs, drug substances, or potential drugs or drug substances of natural origin.
According to a recent WHO report, about 80\% of world population is still using natural product for their primary healthcare needs.
The chief ingredient of aspirin, salicylic acid, was originally obtained from the bark of willow tree.
Quinine, a drug used to treat malaria, is obtained from the bark of cinchona tree, $\textit{Cinchona}$ sp.
Digitalis, used in treatment of heart disease, is obtained from the leaves of foxglove plant, $\textit{Digitalis\ purpurea}$.
Periwinkle, $\textit{Catharanthus}$ sp., is the source of two drugs that are effective against Hodgkin disease and some leukemia.
Morphine and codeine are extracted from opium poppy, $\textit{Papaver\ somniferum}$.
Over 50\% of commercially available drugs are based on bioactive compounds extracted from non-human species.
The healing of human minor illness by using therapies based on medicines obtained from animals or ultimately derived from them is known as zootherapy.
Leeches ($\textit{Hirudo\ medicinalis}$) are approved by the Food and Drug Administration for medical use.
Leech feeding on the surface of the skin secretes anti-coagulants which helps to keep the blood flowing.
Bradykinins from the viper venom were eventually developed into the drug captopril, used to treat hypertension.
Maggots can turn a chronic wound into an acute wound in a matter of days by eating the chronic tissue and bacteria.
Spider silk has super tensile strength and can be used to make artificial ligaments and tendons.
The spider silk gene for the silk protein is connected to DNA in the mammary gland of goat.
Salmon produce calcitonin hormones to regulate their own calcium levels from an endocrine gland in their neck.
Synthetic version of calcitonin from coho salmon can be used to treat people with calcium regulation disorders.
A protein in the blood of the horseshoe crab called Limulus Amebocyte Lysate (LAL) reacts to all kinds of microorganisms and can easily detect dangerous endotoxins.
Plant fibres are sclerenchymatous cells and are parts of the plant skeleton.
Linen, nylon, and fabric dyes are derived from the plants.
Polyester is produced from fossils.
Cotton ($\textit{Gossypium}$ spp.) is the world's chief fibre plant and the industrial crop of Myanmar.
Jute ($\textit{Corchorus}$ spp.) is a tropical plant that grows best in hot and humid climate.
Sericulture involves rearing of silkworm moths, $\textit{Bombyx\ mori}$, for the production of raw silk.
Silk is known as the Queen of Textiles.
Silk is a continuous protein filament from the cocoon.
Wool is the textile fibre obtained from sheep and other mammals, especially goats, rabbits, and camelids.
Wool consists of protein together with a small percentage of lipids.
Wood ranks next to food and fibres as the most widely used plant product.
Unprocessed wood may be used in the form of lumber, which is wood intended for heavy construction.
Structural timbers are obtained mainly from the softwoods and from the heart of the tree.
Teak ($\textit{Tectona\ grandis}$) and Pyinkado ($\textit{Xylia\ dolabriformis}$) are the most valuable of timber yielding plants of Myanmar.
